methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

data.narrative:
Skyryse, a Series C space and aerospace company, is valued at $1.15B post-money and has raised $500M+. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 4 same-stage private peers (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Isar Aerospace) valued between $1.0B and $2.5B, alongside 5 public comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector.

Two methods triangulate to a defensible range. The peer-median approach anchors to $1.6B, reflecting the median valuation of the 4 closest private comparables in sector and stage. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the current $1.1B post-money valuation, implying $2.0B at next stage. These methods span from $1.6B to $2.0B, with a blended midpoint of $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 59.8x across 6 tickers) is not applicable due to lack of disclosed revenue data for the target.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B to $2.2B, with a median of $1.8B. This places Skyryse at the lower end of the peer distribution, consistent with its current post-money valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and near-term funding environment, both material to stage-progression assumptions and peer re-rating. The current $1.15B valuation sits defensibly within the $1.4B–$2.2B range.
